evidence: Ownership inversion around media models: many `MediaGalleryItem/*` modules and `MediaGallery/services/downloadService.ts` import `GeneratedImageWithMetadata`/`SimplifiedShotOption` from `MediaGallery/types` (for example `MediaGalleryItem/hooks/useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`, `MediaGalleryItem/components/InfoTooltip.tsx`, `MediaGallery/services/downloadService.ts`), making sibling/leaf modules depend on a parent feature package.

`useProjectCRUD` combines persistence (`getSupabaseClient`), cross-service setup (`projectSetupService`), and UI presentation (`toast`, `normalizeAndPresentError`) in one shared hook, and that mixed contract is then funneled through `ProjectContext` via `useProjectSessionCoordinator`, increasing cross-layer coupling.

A generic open-state contract already exists in `src/shared/components/dialogs/contracts.ts` (`OpenStateContract`), but generation domain defines `DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` with the same shape, creating parallel contract surfaces for the same boundary pattern.

Mechanical concern signal `holistic_context.coupling.module_level_io` appears mostly noisy for this batch (many hits are generic `open` prop references, not true I/O pressure), but it did point to a real architecture smell: repeated ad hoc open-state contracts across modules.

src/pages/ArtPage.tsx:49-53 builds Tailwind utility classes via template strings (`from-${piece.color}/20`, `text-${piece.color}`), while the rest of the codebase predominantly uses static class tokens; this creates a style island and brittle build-time class discovery.

src/shared/components/DecorativeBackground.tsx:1 exports a component named as a visual primitive but always returns `null`; src/shared/components/ReighLoading.tsx:11 renders it as if it has behavior, which violates naming/behavior expectations.

Mechanical convention signal `duplicate_clusters` was reviewed for nearby seed areas: the reported duplication around `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/reposition/*` is detector-level boilerplate and did not surface a material convention defect in inspected files.

src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts mixes failure contracts in one flow: `createTaskOrThrow` hard-fails (`throw new Error(...)` at 181), but metadata failures in `saveEnhancedPromptMetadata` are normalized then silently returned (204-205, 220-221), producing partial success without caller-level failure signaling.

Storage boundary errors are handled inconsistently: src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptManagement/storedPromptCount.ts:11-13 and usePromptPersistenceEffects.ts:44-46,65-67 swallow exceptions silently, while src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/referenceUpload/optimisticCacheUpdate.ts:6-10 normalizes and reports.

Provider-hook boundary errors use divergent strategy in same layer: src/shared/contexts/AuthContext.tsx:160-163 and UserSettingsContext.tsx:155-158 log+throw, but src/shared/contexts/ProjectContext.tsx:44-49 throws directly with no normalized reporting.
Mechanical `errors.strategy_by_directory` is confirmed by sampled code: `promptManagement/` and `contexts/` exhibit mixed catch/swallow/throw patterns rather than a single intentional contract.

Mechanical indirection hotspots are confirmed in current code: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx` builds a large adapter object (`buildImageSharedLightboxInput`) and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/hooks/useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` builds another large render model over shared state, creating multi-layer mapping before UI rendering.

Mechanical wide-param-bag signals are confirmed: travel task payload shaping is spread across `src/shared/lib/tasks/travelBetweenImages/payloadBuilder.ts`, `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts`, and `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/applySettings/taskDataService.ts`, each with its own precedence/fallback rules.

The codebase already has a shared abstraction for travel payload reads (`src/shared/lib/tasks/travelPayloadReader.ts`), but adjacent modules still implement custom field precedence logic, indicating abstraction bypass rather than missing primitives.

Pass-through wrapper signals are mixed: some wrappers are low-cost aliases (for example `useUpdatingTimestamp` in `src/shared/hooks/useUpdatingTimestamp.ts`), while hotspots are dominated by high-cardinality state adapters rather than trivial wrappers.

No additional delegation-heavy classes, facade-only modules, or TypedDict violations were found in this batch scope; packet values (`delegation_heavy_class_count=0`, `facade_module_count=0`, `typed_dict_violation_count=0`) are consistent with sampled files.

complete_task mixes two incompatible error contracts: edge-style envelope from request parser (errorCode/recoverable) and plain jsonResponse({ error }) paths in handler branches, so callers cannot rely on one schema.
Shared edge bootstrap layer is widely used (`bootstrapEdgeHandler` in multiple functions), but direct test coverage is missing for edgeHandler behavior (preflight/method/auth/format choreography).

Mechanical signal_density concern on `src/shared/lib/tasks/taskParamContract.ts` is confirmed: it is a contract-heavy parser with broad fallback paths and coercion behavior, but no direct tests were found.

Mechanical concern on `src/shared/lib/generationTaskRepository.ts` is partially refuted: there is focused behavior coverage in `src/shared/lib/__tests__/generationTaskRepository.test.ts` for query_failed/scope/missing_generation cases.

Travel-between-images still runs a dual legacy/canonical contract: `types.ts` says canonical payload excludes migration aliases, but UI/state/services still carry and map deprecated fields (`structure_type`, `motion_strength`, `structure_video_*`) across adapters and settings readers.

Migration behavior is inconsistent: `useStructureVideo.migrateToArrayFormat` force-coerces all loaded videos to `structure_type: 'uni3c'`, while other paths (`taskDataService.extractSettings`, `structureGuidance.ts`) continue preserving/deriving `flow|canny|depth`.

Default payload values include test/scaffold-like literals (`model_name: "base_tester_model"`, `debug: true`, `main_output_dir_for_run: "./outputs/default_travel_output"`) and are applied by `payloadBuilder` when params are absent.

Mechanical scan evidence had no explicit concern signals for this batch (`concern_signals: []`); complexity hotspot lead around TimelineContainer was inspected and is mostly complexity/comment density rather than additional migration-defect evidence for this dimension.

Objective structure shows overloaded flat directories: `src/shared/components/` has 50 production files (11,751 LOC), `src/shared/hooks/` has 69 files (13,266 LOC), and `src/shared/lib/` has 67 files (8,498 LOC), with mixed concerns in each.

Coupling concentration is high across these broad folders (`src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/hooks/ = 41`, `src/shared/components/ -> src/shared/lib/ = 22`, `src/shared/hooks/ -> src/shared/lib/ = 51`), indicating weak package boundaries rather than focused subpackages.

`src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts` is placed in `lib` but exports a React Query hook (`useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`) and is consumed from UI hook layers (`src/shared/components/TaskDetails/hooks/useGenerationTaskDetails.ts`), confirming a layer-placement mismatch.

Root-level `src/shared/components/` contains UI components alongside non-component domain/service files (`segmentSettingsMigration.ts`, `segmentSettingsMigrationLegacy.ts`, `segmentSettingsUtils.ts`, `useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts`), reducing navigability and ownership clarity.

Seed-file mechanical signal check: `holistic_context.scan_evidence.systemic_patterns.async_no_await` includes `src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts`; this is confirmed as a real hotspot file, and in this batch it aligns with a package-boundary issue (hook in utility package), not just an async-style concern.

Global bootstrap contract is hard: `getSupabaseClient()` throws until `initializeSupabase()` has run (`src/integrations/supabase/client.ts`), and app startup order in `src/app/bootstrap.tsx` is the only initializer path.

Runtime uses module singletons (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient` and `runtimeClient`) across `src/integrations/supabase/client.ts` and `src/integrations/supabase/runtime/supabaseRuntime.ts`, coupling feature code to import-time runtime state.
Many production modules call `getSupabaseClient()` directly (hooks/libs/services), so business logic is not executable without global runtime setup.

Packet-required mutable-global evidence is incomplete: `holistic_context.scan_evidence.mutable_globals` and `holistic_context.errors.mutable_globals` are null in this packet; used `systemic_patterns.window_global` plus direct code inspection as fallback evidence.

Confirmed signal: `src/pages/Home/components/panes/PhilosophyPane.tsx` is 627 LOC and mixes content authoring, media preload behavior, travel-demo layout branching, and control wiring in one component.

Confirmed signal: `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/StrokeOverlay.tsx` is 527 LOC with a large prop surface and combines pointer interaction state machine, geometry transforms, imperative API (`exportMask/undo/clear/deleteSelected/toggleFreeForm`), and rendering.

Confirmed signal: `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/formSubmission/submissionTaskPlan.ts` uses a 17-field `SubmissionTaskContext`; adjacent `useFormSubmission` and `MediaGalleryLightbox` also use wide orchestration contracts, indicating repeated context-bag design.
Confirmed signal: `src/domains/generation/hooks/useGenerationMutations.ts` still has high branch density in `useDeleteFromTable` and optimistic cache choreography in `useToggleGenerationStar`.
Refuted signal: `src/domains/generation/mappers/generationParamsMapper.ts` complexity concern is largely addressed by table-driven `FIELD_MAPPINGS` plus shared helpers; control flow is mostly linear.

Refuted signal: `src/shared/components/GlobalHeader/ProjectSelectorPopover.tsx` `handleKeyDown` signature variance is benign event typing (`React.KeyboardEvent`) and not a design defect in this module.

Refuted signal: `src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/components/reference/ReferenceThumbnail.tsx` touch handler signature variance is routine React touch-event usage, not structural incoherence.

Refuted signal: `src/shared/components/LoraSelectorModal/utils/validation-utils.ts` `generateUniqueFilename` variance is cross-domain naming collision (`storagePaths` vs LoRA naming utility), not an internal design flaw in this file.

src/domains/generation/types/index.ts documents itself as the stable public type surface, but it exports `Shot` from `./shot` even though `src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts` does not define `Shot`.

Because the barrel does not provide the needed `Shot` shape, consumers bypass it and import `Shot` directly from `src/domains/generation/types/shotViewRow.ts` (e.g., src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotEditor/services/generateVideo/types.ts), contradicting the stated contract boundary.

src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptManagement/types.ts declares `handleAddPrompt: (source?: 'form' | 'modal') => void`, but implementation returned from src/shared/components/ImageGenerationForm/hooks/promptManagement/usePromptHandlers.ts and forwarded by usePromptManagement does not accept or use this argument.

findings.summary: MediaGalleryItem and gallery services depend on MediaGallery’s internal type module.
findings.suggestion: Extract gallery media model types into a neutral module (for example `src/shared/media/contracts` or `src/features/gallery/contracts`) and update both `MediaGallery` and `MediaGalleryItem` to consume that shared contract instead of importing from `MediaGallery/types`.
evidence: `useMediaGalleryItemState.ts`, `InfoTooltip.tsx`, `ShotActions.tsx`, and other `MediaGalleryItem` files import from `../../MediaGallery/types`.
`downloadService.ts` imports `GeneratedImageWithMetadata` from `@/shared/components/MediaGallery/types` despite being a service used across gallery interactions.
`MediaGalleryItem/types.ts` re-exports types from `../MediaGallery/types`, further spreading parent-feature ownership outward.

findings.summary: Project CRUD hook mixes data access, orchestration, and UI error/toast presentation.
findings.suggestion: Split `useProjectCRUD` into: (1) a data/repository layer that returns typed results/errors with no UI side effects, and (2) a UI adapter in `ProjectContext` (or a presenter hook) that maps those results to `toast`/runtime-error presentation.
evidence: `useProjectCRUD.ts` imports `getSupabaseClient` (infra), `projectSetupService` (service orchestration), and UI presentation (`toast`, `normalizeAndPresentError`) in the same hook.
The hook directly emits UI toasts (`toast.error(...)`) and presentation errors while also performing DB CRUD and setup transactions.
`ProjectContext` composes `selection + crud + identity`, so this mixed hook becomes a broad app-level dependency surface.

findings.summary: Open-state dialog contract is duplicated between shared UI and generation domain.
findings.suggestion: Standardize on `OpenStateContract` as the base dialog contract and compose domain-specific fields on top of it (for example `type DeleteGenerationConfirmContract = OpenStateContract & { onConfirm: () => void }`).
evidence: `OpenStateContract` already defines `{ open, onOpenChange }` in shared dialogs contracts.
`DeleteGenerationConfirmContract` redefines the same `open`/`onOpenChange` shape in the generation domain.
Other modules (for example `InstallInstructionsModal`) already consume the shared open-state contract, so this is inconsistent boundary typing.

findings.summary: Segment submission path mixes hard-fail and soft-fail semantics for adjacent operations.
findings.suggestion: Define one contract for `submitSegmentTask` sub-steps (either fail-fast with rethrow or explicit typed partial-result), then apply it consistently to prompt-enhancement metadata writes and task creation so callers can reliably react to failure states.
evidence: src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts:181 throws when task creation lacks `task_id`.
src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/submitSegmentTask.ts:204-205 and 220-221 normalize metadata errors then return, allowing task creation to continue after persistence failure.
src/shared/lib/errorHandling/runtimeError.ts:26-28 already provides `normalizeAndPresentAndRethrow`, but this path does not use a consistent rethrow-or-result contract.

findings.summary: Storage I/O errors are silently swallowed in some paths but surfaced in others.
findings.suggestion: Create a shared storage error policy helper (e.g., `safeStorageRead/safeStorageWrite`) that always captures context and returns typed fallback values; replace ad-hoc silent catches so storage failures are observable and behavior stays predictable.
evidence: storedPromptCount.ts:11-13 catches sessionStorage errors and suppresses them without logging/metrics.
usePromptPersistenceEffects.ts:44-46 and 65-67 suppress storage write failures in two effects.
projectSelectionStore.ts:22-23 suppresses localStorage read failures.
optimisticCacheUpdate.ts:6-10 normalizes and reports caught exceptions, showing a conflicting strategy for similar client-side boundary failures.

findings.summary: Art page uses runtime-generated Tailwind class tokens unlike the static class convention.
findings.suggestion: Replace interpolated utility tokens with a static variant map (`color -> className bundle`) so all classes are explicit and discoverable at build time, and enforce the map type against allowed palette values.
evidence: ArtPage.tsx:49-53 constructs class names with template interpolation (`from-${piece.color}/20`, `border-${piece.color}/40`, `text-${piece.color}`), which depends on runtime values.
artPieces.ts:21,31,41,51,61,71 provides color keys as data values, coupling styling correctness to data strings rather than explicit style mapping.

findings.summary: A named visual component is a permanent no-op, creating misleading API semantics.
findings.suggestion: Either implement the component’s intended visuals or remove the abstraction and call site; if it is intentionally feature-flagged off, rename to an explicit stub (e.g., `DecorativeBackgroundStub`) and document deprecation/removal path.
evidence: DecorativeBackground.tsx:1 exports `DecorativeBackground` but returns `null` unconditionally.
ReighLoading.tsx:11 renders `<DecorativeBackground />` as if it contributes UI behavior, making the call site misleading during maintenance and reviews.

findings.summary: Lightbox state is repeatedly reshaped through oversized adapter layers before rendering
findings.suggestion: Collapse to a smaller domain model boundary: extract a `LightboxDomainState` with stable selectors and move consumer-specific mapping into small selector helpers; remove manual whole-object remapping in `buildImageSharedLightboxInput`/`useVideoLightboxRenderModel` and expose only feature slices each panel needs.
evidence: `useSharedLightboxState` defines a very wide input/output contract with nested sections (variants/navigation/star/references/lineage/shots/source/layout/button groups), then consumers rebuild more derived models on top.
`ImageLightbox.tsx` adds another adapter (`buildImageSharedLightboxInput`) that manually maps many fields from `props` and `env` into the shared contract.
`useVideoLightboxRenderModel.tsx` builds additional memoized objects (`lightboxVariants`, `lightboxStateValue`) that largely repackage `sharedState` again.

findings.summary: Inline edit hook returns a mega DTO that forces downstream remapping and coupling
findings.suggestion: Split `useInlineEditState` into focused hooks (`useInlineEditCanvasModel`, `useInlineEditGenerationActions`, `useInlineEditVariants`) and have the view consume those directly; keep `ImageEditProvider` payload minimal and derived close to usage sites.
evidence: `useInlineEditState` assembles a very large `InlineEditStateResult` plus `imageEditValue` with many pass-through members from multiple sub-hooks.
`InlineEditView.tsx` immediately performs further projection via `buildInlineEditCanvasModel` and `buildInlineEditSidebarModel`, indicating the upstream contract is broader than each consumer needs.
The state surface couples canvas behavior, generation actions, variant management, prompt settings, transform state, and UI mode state in one return object.

findings.summary: Travel task payload precedence is duplicated across writer/reader modules with divergent rules
findings.suggestion: Create one canonical travel contract module that owns read/write precedence for shared fields and enforce its use in task creation, segment regeneration, and apply-settings extraction; move precedence tables into typed constants and remove local ad hoc fallback chains.
evidence: `payloadBuilder.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts` each perform extensive task-param shaping and fallback resolution for overlapping fields (prompts, frames, model/motion config, structure video, loras).
`taskDataService.ts` reimplements another precedence chain (`orchestrator` vs `params` vs structure-guidance fields) instead of relying on shared reader utilities.
Shared abstractions exist (`createTravelPayloadReader` and payload snapshot helpers), but are not consistently used, so precedence policy is spread and drifts by call site.

findings.summary: complete_task returns mixed error schemas across failure paths
findings.suggestion: Standardize all complete_task error exits on a single edge envelope. Introduce a local helper (or reuse `_shared/edgeRequest.edgeErrorResponse`) and replace `jsonResponse({ error: ... })` branches with explicit `{ errorCode, message, recoverable }` responses so clients can parse failures uniformly.
evidence: `parseCompleteTaskRequest` returns `edgeErrorResponse(...)` with `error`, `errorCode`, `message`, `recoverable`.

`completeTaskHandler` returns plain `jsonResponse({ error: ... }, status)` in multiple branches (auth failure, security failure, actor policy failure, not-found, internal errors), dropping `errorCode` and `recoverable` fields.
The same endpoint therefore exposes two different error contracts depending on which branch fails.

findings.summary: generation-core mutating DB helpers only have export-shape tests
findings.suggestion: Replace export-only tests with behavior tests using mocked Supabase responses for success and failure branches of each helper (`findExistingGeneration`, `findSourceGenerationByImageUrl`, `insertGeneration`, `createVariant`, `linkGenerationToShot`). Assert expected query methods, status handling (`PGRST116`), thrown errors, and non-throw best-effort paths.
evidence: `generation-core.ts` contains DB behavior (contains/single/maybeSingle/insert/rpc) and error handling branches.
`generation-core.test.ts` only asserts each export is a function and does not verify query shape, error handling, or return semantics.
These helpers are used in completion flow modules (`generation.ts`, `generation-handlers.ts`, `generation-child.ts`), so contract regressions would be high-impact and currently hard to catch.

findings.summary: task param extractors coerce arbitrary values to strings, masking bad payload shapes
findings.suggestion: Change string extractors to return `null` unless the resolved value is a non-empty string (optionally UUID-validate where required), and add explicit validation errors at call sites that require strict IDs. Add unit tests for mixed-type payloads to lock in strict behavior.
evidence: `extractTaskParamString` returns `String(value)` for any non-null value instead of enforcing string type.
Contract consumers (`extractRunIdParam`, `extractShotIdParam`, `extractBasedOnParam`) inherit that coercion and are used by complete_task parameter extraction.
Malformed values (objects/arrays/numbers) become stringified identifiers (for example `[object Object]`), which hides contract violations and can propagate invalid IDs into downstream logic.

findings.summary: core edge bootstrap/actor-policy contracts lack direct behavioral tests
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests for `bootstrapEdgeHandler`/`withEdgeRequest` and `resolveTaskStorageActor` covering success and policy-failure branches (service-role bypass, user ownership denial, method mismatch, strict/loose parse modes, JSON vs text error format). This should be treated as shared contract tests reused for all edge functions depending on these modules.
evidence: `edgeHandler.ts` defines shared contract choreography (method enforcement, JSON parse mode, auth policy, error format), and is used by many functions.
`taskActorPolicy.ts` centralizes ownership + service-role storage actor resolution used by complete_task.
Search in test/spec files found coverage for `edgeRequest` and `rateLimit`, but no direct tests for `edgeHandler` or `taskActorPolicy`, leaving shared contract behavior unguarded.

findings.summary: Travel structure migration keeps both canonical and legacy payload paths active in core runtime code
findings.suggestion: Finish one migration boundary: keep legacy parsing only in a single adapter at task ingress/egress, then remove legacy fields from UI/editor models and service composition paths. Enforce with one canonical `StructureGuidance` DTO used by settings, modal generation, and payload builder.
evidence: `types.ts` documents canonical payloads as legacy-free, but `uiTypes.ts` re-introduces `LegacyStructureVideoFields` into editor-facing structures.
`taskDataService.extractSettings` merges legacy and canonical fields from both params and orchestrator (`structure_video_type`, `structure_type`, `structure_video_motion_strength`, etc.).
`VideoGenerationModal` still builds `structureVideos` entries with legacy per-video keys (`motion_strength`, `structure_type`) as primary UI output.

findings.summary: Legacy migration code force-rewrites structure type to uni3c while other modules still support flow/canny/depth
findings.suggestion: Pick one explicit policy and encode it centrally: either remove non-uni3c support everywhere now, or preserve all types until cutoff. Add a single conversion helper with telemetry so coercion is intentional and auditable.
evidence: `useStructureVideo.migrateToArrayFormat` rewrites both array and single-video loads to `structure_type: 'uni3c'` unconditionally.
`taskDataService` and `structureGuidance.ts` still read/map non-uni3c variants (`flow`, `canny`, `depth`) into guidance output.
This creates silent semantic coercion on read/write cycles, which is migration debt disguised as compatibility handling.

findings.summary: Runtime defaults include placeholder/test-like values that leak into production payload construction
findings.suggestion: Replace scaffold literals with production-safe defaults (or make these fields required at call boundary). Split test-only defaults into test fixtures and fail validation when runtime-critical fields are missing.
evidence: `DEFAULT_TRAVEL_BETWEEN_IMAGES_VALUES` contains `model_name: "base_tester_model"`, `debug: true`, and `main_output_dir_for_run: "./outputs/default_travel_output"`.
`payloadBuilder` applies these defaults when fields are omitted (`model_name`, `debug_mode_enabled`, `main_output_dir_for_run`).
`createTravelBetweenImagesTask` does not enforce caller-supplied model/debug/output-dir, so fallback literals can reach persisted orchestrator payloads.

findings.summary: Generation type module contains an unexported, unused UI row type artifact
findings.suggestion: Remove `GenerationUiRow` and its private overlay interface, or export and adopt it where optimistic UI state truly needs it. Keep domain type modules free of dead compatibility artifacts.
evidence: `GenerationUiRow` is defined in `generationViewRow.ts` but never exported or referenced.
`types/index.ts` exports only `GenerationRow`, leaving `GenerationUiRow` as dead migration/experimentation residue in the canonical types module.

findings.summary: `src/shared/lib` contains React hooks and React Query orchestration, breaking the expected utility-layer boundary and obscuring ownership.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create focused folders `src/shared/hooks/taskMappings/`, `src/shared/hooks/events/`, and `src/shared/hooks/debug/`; 2) move `generationTaskBridge.ts`, `typedEvents.ts` hook export, and `debug/debugRendering.ts` hook exports into hook packages while keeping pure non-hook utilities in `lib`; 3) split mixed modules where needed (for example keep `dispatchAppEvent`/`listenAppEvent` in `src/shared/lib/events/typedEvents.ts` and move only `useAppEventListener` to hooks); 4) update imports with `desloppify move src/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge.ts src/shared/hooks/taskMappings/useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration.ts --dry-run` (repeat per file), then apply; 5) validate with `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and targeted tests such as `pnpm vitest src/shared/lib/__tests__/generationTaskBridge.test.ts src/shared/lib/__tests__/typedEvents.test.ts`.
evidence: `generationTaskBridge.ts` imports `useMutation` and exports `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` from `src/shared/lib/`.
`typedEvents.ts` exports `useAppEventListener` with `useEffect` from `src/shared/lib/`.
`debugRendering.ts` exports `useRenderLogger` and `useChangedDepsLogger` from `src/shared/lib/`.
A UI hook (`useGenerationTaskDetails.ts`) imports `useGetPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration` from `@/shared/lib/generationTaskBridge`, reinforcing cross-layer ambiguity.

findings.summary: The flat `src/shared/components/` root is overloaded with unrelated UI, migration, and view-model files, weakening directory boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) introduce purpose-driven subfolders under `src/shared/components/` such as `modals/`, `view-models/`, and move migration logic out to `src/shared/lib/segment-settings/`; 2) move compatibility files first (`segmentSettingsMigration*.ts`, `segmentSettingsUtils.ts`) to avoid circular drift; 3) move `useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts` to `src/shared/hooks/view-models/`; 4) move modal components to `src/shared/components/modals/`; 5) update import paths via `desloppify move <src> <dst> --dry-run`, then run `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and `pnpm vitest src/shared/components/**/*.test.tsx src/shared/components/**/*.test.ts`.
evidence: Directory profile reports `src/shared/components/` at 50 files and 11,751 LOC, exceeding the mixed-concern threshold in the packet guidance.
Files in this folder combine pure components (`CreateShotModal.tsx`) with migration/compatibility logic (`segmentSettingsMigration.ts`) and view-model hooks (`useImageGenerationDetailsViewModel.ts`).
Top coupling from this directory spans UI primitives, hooks, and libs (`-> ui = 84`, `-> hooks = 41`, `-> lib = 22`), showing it functions as a catch-all package.

findings.summary: Domain-specific resource/shot modals are parked in shared root paths instead of domain packages, despite narrow importer affinity.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) create a feature package for resource browsing and shot creation (for example `src/features/resources/components/` and `src/features/shots/components/` or equivalent domain folders); 2) move `DatasetBrowserModal.tsx` first with its tests, then move `CreateShotModal.tsx`; 3) leave temporary re-export shims in old paths for one migration cycle to reduce churn; 4) bulk-update imports (`rg -l "@/shared/components/(CreateShotModal|DatasetBrowserModal)" src`) and replace to new aliases; 5) remove shims after `pnpm -s tsc --noEmit` and relevant tests (`pnpm vitest src/shared/components/DatasetBrowserModal.test.tsx src/shared/components/CreateShotModal.test.tsx`).
evidence: `CreateShotModal.tsx` is primarily consumed by image-generation and shot/travel flows (`ImageGenerationForm.tsx`, `ShotListView.tsx`, `MediaGalleryItem.tsx`) rather than as a broadly reusable primitive.

`DatasetBrowserModal.tsx` is consumed by reference/structure-video workflows (`ReferenceSection.tsx`, `StructureVideoSection.tsx`, `BatchGuidanceVideo.tsx`, `TimelineContainer.tsx`) and internally couples to resource hooks plus Supabase session access.
Both modules live in `src/shared/components/` root, which blurs boundary ownership between shared primitives and workflow/domain UI.

findings.summary: Video play listeners are added per clip without valid React cleanup, causing leaked handlers.
findings.suggestion: Refactor autoplay prevention into a dedicated effect that tracks attached elements and returns a single top-level cleanup function removing every registered listener (or push this into `SortableClip` with element-local cleanup).
evidence: In `useClipManager` autoplay effect, `clips.forEach(...)` adds `video.addEventListener('play', preventPlay)` and returns cleanup from inside the `forEach` callback; React ignores those inner returns, so listeners are never removed.
This hook is mounted by `JoinClipsPage`, so each clip list refresh can accumulate stale `play` handlers on reused DOM nodes.

findings.summary: Async clip hydration can commit stale state after project switches.
findings.suggestion: Add a per-run token or `AbortController` in initialization: capture `selectedProjectId` at effect start and ignore async completion if it changed; also centralize project-switch hydration in a small state machine to make ordering explicit.
evidence: `useClipManager` initialization effect starts async preload (`preloadPosterImages(...).then(...)`) and then sets `clips`/`initialHydrationComplete` without checking that `selectedProjectId` is still current.
Project change reset logic runs in a separate effect, but the earlier async chain has no cancellation/sequence guard, so old project preload completion can overwrite new project initialization state.

findings.summary: Lightbox logic is fragmented into pass-through facades with high indirection and weak payoff.
findings.suggestion: Collapse facade layers around explicit domain slices (navigation, variants, shot-actions) with narrower typed contracts, and remove adapter functions that only rename/relay values; keep one composition boundary near UI rendering.
evidence: `ImageLightbox` constructs large adapter objects (`buildImageSharedLightboxInput`, render models, workflow models) that mostly relay the same values across layers.

`useSharedLightboxState` re-splits and reassembles section state (`useSharedVariantsState`, `useSharedLightboxInteractionState`, `useLightboxPanelModel`) while still exposing broad composite contracts, increasing dependency surface and update churn.

findings.summary: Shot editor controller remains a monolithic orchestration hub with unstable seams.
findings.suggestion: Split controller into a small coordinator plus scenario-specific modules (generation run-state, media-editing state, join workflow state) and replace ref-based dependency bypass with explicit event interfaces between modules.
evidence: `useShotEditorController` is ~846 LOC and coordinates many concerns (settings providers, join workflow, generation, media editing, image management, navigation, parent bridge refs) in one hook.
Frequent `useRef` indirection (`actionsRef`, mutation refs, `selectedShotIdRef`) is used to stabilize callbacks across broad dependency surfaces, signaling that boundaries are too coupled.
This mirrors the same composition style seen in shared lightbox state: large orchestrators with many cross-cutting inputs instead of narrow, behavior-focused modules.

findings.summary: Supabase contract docs describe bootstrap/runtime APIs that do not exist in code.
findings.suggestion: Choose one canonical runtime contract and align both sides in one change set: (1) either add `src/app/runtime/initRuntimes.ts` + `hasInitializedSupabaseClientRuntime()` and migrate bootstrap, or (2) rewrite the doc to the actual `initializeSupabase()` flow and remove stale API claims. Then validate with `npm run test:contracts && npm run check:contracts`.
evidence: Doc claims bootstrap calls `initializeSupabaseClientRuntime()` from `src/app/runtime/initRuntimes.ts`, but that file path is absent and bootstrap calls `initializeSupabase()` directly.
Doc lists `hasInitializedSupabaseClientRuntime()` as runtime-readiness API, but no such symbol exists in `src/`.
This creates docs-vs-runtime contract drift for architecture/quality gates named in the same doc.

findings.summary: Legacy Supabase usage budget is tied to the canonical import path, making governance metrics non-discriminating.
findings.suggestion: Retarget deprecation policy to a real legacy specifier/module (or retire the policy if migration completed). Update `legacySupabasePolicy.ts`, adjust contract tests to scan only the legacy path, and keep canonical `@/integrations/supabase/client` out of legacy-budget accounting. Re-run `npm run test:contracts` and `npm run check:legacy-supabase-usage`.
evidence: `LEGACY_SUPABASE_ALIAS_SPECIFIER` is `@/integrations/supabase/client`.
The same path is documented as canonical runtime entrypoint in Supabase contract docs.
Importer-budget test scans for this specifier and treats matches as legacy-facade usage, so it cannot distinguish true legacy imports from approved canonical imports.

findings.summary: Supabase initialization status is tracked in two modules, creating split authority.
findings.suggestion: Converge on one source of truth in `supabaseRuntime.ts`: export a `hasInitializedSupabaseClientRuntime()` accessor and make `getSupabaseClientResult()` derive readiness from runtime state instead of a second boolean. Validate via `npm run test:contracts` and existing Supabase runtime tests.
evidence: `client.ts` guards access via local boolean `hasInitializedRuntimeClient`.
`supabaseRuntime.ts` separately stores the real singleton in `runtimeClient`.

If future call paths initialize runtime through `initializeSupabaseClientRuntime()` directly, `getSupabaseClientResult()` can still return not-initialized because it only trusts the boolean in another module.

findings.summary: Unused compatibility barrels remain in production paths and fragment canonical package surfaces.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) move these `src/types/*` compatibility shims into `src/domains/generation/compat/` as explicit transitional aliases, 2) add a single `compat/index.ts` with deprecation metadata, 3) update any remaining external/docs references to canonical `@/domains/generation/types`, 4) remove compat files once import count is zero. Validation: `rg "@/types/(generationRow|shot|generationAndShots)" src`, then `npm run test:contracts && npm run quality:check`.
evidence: Mechanical scan already flags `src/types/generationAndShots.ts` as orphaned dead code.
Targeted import search found no in-repo importers for `@/types/generationRow`, `@/types/shot`, or `@/types/generationAndShots`.
Canonical generation type surface is already concentrated at `src/domains/generation/types/index.ts` with high fan-in.

findings.summary: UI contracts folder includes an unused re-export wrapper that adds navigation noise.
findings.suggestion: Staged reorg plan: 1) remove `contracts/button.tsx` or relocate it to an explicit `compat/` folder if external consumers still depend on it, 2) keep `contracts/` only for stable low-level contracts like `cn.ts`, 3) enforce import path policy (`@/shared/components/ui/button`) via lint rule or contract test. Validation: `rg "@/shared/components/ui/contracts/button" src`, then `npm run test:contracts`.
evidence: `src/shared/components/ui/contracts/button.tsx` only re-exports `Button`/`buttonVariants` from `ui/button`.
Import search shows no in-repo imports of `@/shared/components/ui/contracts/button`.
`contracts` folder is otherwise used for true shared helper contract (`cn.ts`), so this wrapper mixes distinct purposes in a tiny package.

findings.summary: Editing UI modules combine too many responsibilities in single files/hooks.
findings.suggestion: Split each module by role: extract pure domain reducers/selectors (selection math, stroke geometry, validation), move media asset/config data to separate config files, and keep React components/hooks as thin orchestration layers.
evidence: `StrokeOverlay` contains interaction state machine, keyboard/pointer lifecycle, geometry transforms, rendering, and imperative export/undo API in one 527-LOC module.
`useVideoEditing` combines selection lifecycle, validation, LoRA integration, settings sync, and generation-task triggering in one 546-LOC hook.
`PhilosophyPane` folds static storytelling content and heavy media behavior/layout branching into a 627-LOC component.

findings.summary: Wide context/props bags create opaque call contracts and choreography overhead.
findings.suggestion: Introduce smaller intent-specific contracts (e.g., `ReferenceGenerationInputs`, `PromptBatchInputs`, `ShotAssignmentInputs`) and compose them at call sites; replace pass-through bag layers with focused adapters per workflow.
evidence: `SubmissionTaskContext` has 17 fields and is passed as a generic bag into `buildSubmissionTaskParams`.
`useFormSubmission` destructures and forwards a broad prop surface into multiple orchestration hooks and builders, increasing hidden coupling.

`MediaGalleryLightbox` uses nested contract objects (`core/navigation/actions/shotWorkflow/ui/optimistic/taskModal/taskData/integration`) and then re-flattens them internally, indicating abstraction layers that mostly shuttle data.

findings.summary: Starred-state synchronization logic is duplicated across mutation and view layers.
findings.suggestion: Centralize starred-state cache policy in one query utility/domain service (single optimistic updater + selector), then consume that in both mutation and lightbox view code.
evidence: `useToggleGenerationStar` manually updates multiple cache families (`unified`, `shots`, and shot-specific queries) with custom rollback maps.
`MediaGalleryLightbox` subscribes to query-cache updates and rescans query data to reconstruct current `starred` state, adding local `cacheVersion` rerender plumbing.
Both files encode overlapping cache-consistency policy, which raises drift risk when query key shapes evolve.

findings.summary: Supabase access relies on global runtime initialization order rather than explicit dependency flow.
findings.suggestion: Replace hidden singleton dependency with an explicit runtime registry/context passed into services (or a required provider hook), and keep `getSupabaseClient` as a thin compatibility shim during migration.
evidence: `getSupabaseClient()` throws until `initializeSupabase()` sets runtime state; this is a process-wide mutable bootstrap gate.
Runtime singleton state is spread across module-level vars (`hasInitializedRuntimeClient`, `runtimeClient`).
`initializeSupabase()` is invoked from app bootstrap, while many hooks/services call `getSupabaseClient()` directly, making those modules startup-order dependent.
`logger.flushLogs()` dynamically imports and calls `getSupabaseClient()`; if invoked before bootstrap completion it degrades to warning/fallback behavior.

findings.summary: Generation type barrel advertises a Shot contract it does not actually own or export coherently.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical `Shot` export path and enforce it: either export `Shot` from `shotViewRow.ts` via `index.ts` (and stop exporting nonexistent `Shot` from `shot.ts`), or rename the two shapes to distinct contracts (e.g., `PersistedShotRow` vs `ShotViewModel`) and update imports to use the intended public barrel consistently.
evidence: `index.ts` comment claims a stable public type surface, but `export type { Shot, ShotOption } from './shot'` points to a module where only `ShotOption` and `PersistedShotRow` are defined.
A parallel `Shot` type is defined in `shotViewRow.ts`, and at least one consumer imports that file directly, indicating the barrel contract is not reliable.

findings.summary: Prompt management type contract declares an argument that runtime handlers ignore.
findings.suggestion: Make the contract honest in one direction: either remove the `source` parameter from `PromptManagementOutput`, or implement source-aware behavior and thread the argument through `usePromptManagement` and downstream callers.
evidence: `PromptManagementOutput.handleAddPrompt` is typed as `(source?: 'form' | 'modal') => void`.
`usePromptHandlers` implements `handleAddPrompt` as a zero-argument callback and `usePromptManagement` returns that function unchanged, so the declared `source` contract is dead and misleading.
